25.reason: [Global variable 'q' is declared in the middle of the file outside any function, which is invalid in C prior to C99 and may cause compilation errors depending on compiler settings. Also, 'front' and 'rear' are global but used only in wide(), causing state retention across calls.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Global variable 'q' is declared in the middle of the file outside any function, which is invalid in C prior to C99 and may cause compilation errors depending on compiler settings. Also, 'front' and 'rear' are global but used only in wide(), causing state retention across calls.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Global variable 'q', 'front', and 'rear' are declared outside any function, but they are used only in 'wide'. However, more critically, 'front' and 'rear' are not reset to 0 before each BFS call, causing incorrect queue state reuse across BFS traversals.]

26.reason: [Same issue as above: global variables declared mid-file. Additionally, these should be local to wide() to reset queue state per BFS call.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Same issue as above: global variables declared mid-file. Additionally, these should be local to wide() to reset queue state per BFS call.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Same as above: 'front' and 'rear' are global and not reinitialized before each BFS, leading to wrong traversal results after the first BFS.]

32.reason: [Condition should be 'front < rear' to avoid accessing beyond enqueued elements. Using '<=' may cause reading uninitialized or stale data.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Condition should be 'front < rear' to avoid accessing beyond enqueued elements. Using '<=' may cause reading uninitialized or stale data.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Condition should be 'front < rear' for a standard queue; using '<=' may cause reading beyond valid queue elements. Also, because 'front' and 'rear' are global and not reset, this loop behaves incorrectly on subsequent calls.]

69.reason: [Using \n in scanf format string can cause input reading issues, especially when the next input is on the same line or when there are trailing newlines. It's safer to omit \n and let scanf skip whitespace automatically.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Using '\n' in scanf format can cause unexpected blocking or input consumption issues; it's unnecessary and may lead to runtime hangs or incorrect parsing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Using '\n' in scanf format can cause unexpected blocking or skipping if input doesn't have trailing newline; better to use "%d %d" without \n.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using \n in scanf format string can cause input reading issues, especially when the next input is on the same line or when there are trailing newlines. It's safer to omit \n and let scanf skip whitespace automatically.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using \n in scanf format string can cause input reading issues, especially if the next input doesn't start immediately after a newline. It may lead to unexpected blocking or skipping inputs.]
